message:
feat(string): add timingsafe_bcmp + timingsafe_memcmp (bd-nx5jz)

OpenBSD-origin constant-time byte comparators (now exposed by glibc
2.39+). Both delegate to a new core::string::timingsafe module that
folds the byte stream branchlessly so execution time is independent
of input contents — defeating timing side-channel attacks on HMAC
tags, session tokens, and password hashes.

* core::string::timingsafe::bcmp — XOR-OR fold normalized to {0, 1}.
* core::string::timingsafe::memcmp — first-difference fold pinned via
  branch-free mask, returns sign(b1 - b2) per memcmp convention.
* abi shims handle n == 0 + NULL pre-checks before slicing into the
  pure-safe core helpers.

23 core unit tests (incl. exhaustive single-byte cross-checks against
std arithmetic) + 10 abi integration tests. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
